title: Rest Gallery
desc: When the barracks dim into false night, the glow lines and sleeping galleries need tending before the next training horn.

steps.text:
The Barracks are loud by reputation and quiet by discipline. At the end of the last drill cycle, the training calls stop, the chitin scrape of movement dies down, and the long sleeping galleries shift into false night. Blue glow cups burn low along the walls. Armor racks darken into ridges of shadow. Rows of Mandiblors settle into wall alcoves and sling rests built for bodies that recover hard and wake quickly. In the dim, the whole chamber seems to breathe as one enormous patient creature.

The gallery keeper carries a tray of fresh glow paste and a roll of woven sling cord. "Refresh the glow cups if you want the light work," she says. "Or retie the sleeping slings along the lower alcoves if you prefer the fabric. Move quietly either way. The barracks can rest, but only if someone keeps the rest orderly."

options.label: Refresh the blue glow cups along the gallery wall
Retie the sleeping slings in the lower alcoves

steps.text:
The glow cups are shallow stone dishes set at even intervals, each one holding a measured smear of bioluminescent paste that keeps the chamber dimly legible without inviting full wakefulness. You clean the soot-dark rims, spread fresh paste with a flat spatula, and shade the brighter cups until the line settles into the low blue band the keeper wants. Once the last cup is reset, the light stops looking patchy and starts flowing cleanly along the wall, enough to mark every alcove, rack, and cross-lane without breaking the hush.

Seen from the center aisle, the galleries no longer feel like military housing at all. They feel like the inside of some living structure at rest, all chitin, breath, and faint blue light. The keeper studies the line and nods. "Better. Even soldiers heal by degrees."

options.label: Set the spatula aside and leave the gallery in low blue light

steps.text:
The sling rests are woven tough enough to hold armored bodies and flexible enough to keep strain off joints worked too hard in the drill lanes. Two have slipped at the wall rings and another has twisted into a knot of bad tension that would leave the next occupant cursing before sleep even started. You loosen each line, pull the weave back into even weight, and knot the side cords so the slings hang level from one anchor to the other.

When you step back, the lower alcoves regain their intended order. Dark forms rest undisturbed above them. Blue light catches the edges of the woven slings and the stacked practice shields beyond, turning the whole gallery into something halfway between barracks and hive chamber. The keeper lowers her voice further, though there is almost no reason to. "Good. Let the next horn be the only rude thing these creatures meet tonight."

options.label: Coil the spare cord and leave the alcoves ready for sleep
